findings.description: The PDF presents the agenda as a three-column layout (Time, Topic, Presenter) without explicit table borders, while the HTML renders it as a formal bordered table. Though the data is preserved, the PDF's layout uses a non-tabular presentation that has been converted into a structured table.
findings.evidence: PDF shows time slots, topics, and presenters arranged in a free-form three-column layout without gridlines. HTML shows a bordered table with explicit 'Time', 'Topic', and 'Presenter' column headers.

findings.description: The PDF does not present the agenda as a formal table with column headers. The HTML converts the agenda items into a table with 'Time', 'Topic', and 'Presenter' column headers, which is a structural interpretation rather than a faithful reproduction. However, all data is accurately represented.
findings.evidence: The PDF uses a formatted list layout with time ranges on the left, topics in the center, and presenters on the right. The HTML adds explicit table headers 'Time', 'Topic', 'Presenter' which don't exist in the PDF.

findings.description: The document has nested <main> elements, which is invalid HTML structure, though this doesn't affect content fidelity. The heading hierarchy uses h1 for 'AGENDA' and h2 for the full meeting title, which is reasonable.
The HTML has duplicate nested <main> elements creating a redundant structural wrapper, though no actual content is duplicated.
findings.evidence: The outer <main> from the template wraps an inner <main> from the converted content.
